paragraphs.text:
Yes! There are many ligands in the NIST SRD-46 database with more than 6 reported pKa values. The count here reflects the total number of individual pKa measurements (across different conditions such as temperature, ionic strength, and literature sources), not necessarily distinct protonation steps.

Here are the **top 20 ligands** ranked by number of reported pKa entries:

| Rank | Ligand ID | Ligand Name (Common Name) | pKa Entries |
|------|-----------|---------------------------|-------------|
| 1 | ligand_8412 | Nitrilotris(methylenephosphonic acid) (NTP) | 30 |
| 2 | ligand_6356 | Diethylenetrinitrilopentaacetic acid (DTPA) | 26 |
| 3 | ligand_8380 | Iminobis(methylenephosphonic acid) (IDP) | 22 |
| 4 | ligand_6277 | Ethylenedinitrilotetraacetic acid (EDTA) | 20 |
| 5 | ligand_9142 | 1-Hydroxyethane-1,1-diphosphonic acid (Etidronic acid) | 18 |
| 6 | ligand_7246 | 1,4,7,10,13,16-Hexaazahexadecane (Pentaethylenehexamine) | 18 |
| 7 | ligand_7244 | 1,4,7,10,13-Pentaazatridecane (Tetraethylenepentamine) | 15 |
| 8 | ligand_10114 | Hydrogen diphosphate (Pyrophosphoric acid) | 14 |
| 9 | ligand_10113 | Hydrogen phosphate (Phosphoric acid) | 14 |
| 10 | ligand_10117 | Hydrogen triphosphate (Triphosphoric acid) | 13 |
| 11 | ligand_8907 | Butanedioic acid (Succinic acid) | 12 |
| 12 | ligand_8873 | Propanedioic acid (Malonic acid) | 12 |
| 13 | ligand_5937 | N-(Phosphonomethyl)glycine (Glyphosate) | 12 |
| 14 | ligand_8872 | Ethanedioic acid (Oxalic acid) | 11 |
| 15 | ligand_9058 | 2-Hydroxypropane-1,2,3-tricarboxylic acid (Citric acid) | 10 |
| 16 | ligand_5898 | L-2-Amino-3-(4-imidazolyl)propanoic acid (Histidine) | 10 |
| 17 | ligand_5760 | Aminoacetic acid (Glycine) | 10 |
| 18 | ligand_10096 | Hydrogen carbonate (Carbonic acid) | 9 |
| 19 | ligand_10077 | Hydrogen vanadate (Vanadic acid) | 9 |
| 20 | ligand_6165 | Nitrilotriacetic acid (NTA) | 9 |

paragraphs.text:
### Chemistry Insights

The leaders in this list make perfect chemical sense. **Nitrilotris(methylenephosphonic acid) (NTP)** tops the list with 30 entries — it has 6 dissociable protons (three phosphonate groups plus a tertiary amine), and its industrial importance in water treatment drives extensive study across varied conditions. **DTPA** (26 entries) has 5 carboxylic acid protons plus 3 amine nitrogens (up to 8 protonation steps), making it one of the most proton-rich chelators in the database. The polyamine ligands like **pentaethylenehexamine** (6 amine groups = 6 distinct pKa steps) also appear prominently, as each amine represents a separate protonation equilibrium. Note that some ligands with fewer dissociable protons (e.g., succinic acid with only 2 pKa steps) still have high entry counts because they have been extensively studied under many different experimental conditions.

*Source: NIST Standard Reference Database 46 (Critically Selected Stability Constants of Metal Complexes).*
